milestones.headline: tits open milk bottles
milestones.title: The opening of milk bottles by birds
milestones.description: Tits learned to pierce the foil caps of doorstep milk bottles, and the cream-stealing behavior spread geographically through the population.

milestones.headline: chimps fish for termites
milestones.silhouetteCredit: T. Michael Keesey & Tony Hisgett · CC BY 3.0
milestones.title: Tool-using and aimed throwing in a community of free-living chimpanzees
milestones.description: Wild chimpanzees at Gombe modify twigs into probes and use them to fish termites from mounds.

milestones.headline: sparrows sing in local dialects
milestones.title: Culturally transmitted patterns of vocal behavior in sparrows
milestones.description: White-crowned sparrows learn songs from local adult tutors, producing discrete, geographically bounded dialects across neighboring populations.

milestones.headline: a macaque invents potato washing
milestones.title: Newly-acquired pre-cultural behavior of the natural troop of Japanese monkeys on Koshima islet
milestones.description: On Koshima Island a juvenile macaque named Imo invented potato-washing in freshwater (and later in seawater), and the behavior spread through her troop along social lines.

milestones.headline: whale songs evolve in unison
milestones.title: Large scale changes over 19 years in songs of humpback whales in Bermuda
milestones.description: Male humpback whales sing the same complex song across an entire population, and the song evolves progressively year over year while remaining synchronized across singers.

milestones.headline: crows craft hook tools
milestones.title: Manufacture and use of hook-tools by New Caledonian crows
milestones.description: Wild New Caledonian crows manufacture standardized hook tools and stepped-cut pandanus tools, with regional design variants across populations.

milestones.headline: chimpanzee cultures, plural
milestones.title: Cultures in chimpanzees
milestones.description: Across seven long-term African field sites, chimpanzee communities have 39 customary or habitual behaviors (including tool use, grooming, and courtship) that vary between groups in ways unexplained by ecology or genetics.

milestones.headline: dolphins forage with sea sponges
milestones.title: Cultural transmission of tool use in bottlenose dolphins
milestones.description: In Shark Bay, bottlenose dolphins wear marine sponges over their beaks as protective foraging tools, a behavior passed almost exclusively from mothers to daughters.

milestones.headline: wild tits conform to traditions
milestones.title: Experimentally induced innovations lead to persistent culture via conformity in wild birds
milestones.description: Experimentally seeded foraging techniques spread through wild great tit social networks via conformity bias and persist across generations, even when alternative solutions are equally rewarding.

milestones.headline: bumblebees copy a two-step puzzle
milestones.title: Bumblebees socially learn behaviour too complex to innovate alone
milestones.description: Buff-tailed bumblebees learn a two-step puzzle box solution from trained demonstrators, a sequence that naive bees almost never discover on their own.
